steps.title: Use vertex form since the maximum is given
steps.content: The problem tells us the maximum daylight is 13.91 hours at $t = 3.5$ months. For a quadratic with a known vertex $(h, k)$, use vertex form: $$d(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$$ where $(h, k) = (3.5, 13.91)$. This gives us: $$d(t) = a(t - 3.5)^2 + 13.91$$

steps.title: Substitute the second point to find a
Find daylight on March 1
steps.content: Now evaluate $d(0)$ to find the daylight on March 1: $$d(0) = -0.2(0 - 3.5)^2 + 13.91$$ $$d(0) = -0.2(12.25) + 13.91$$ $$d(0) = -2.45 + 13.91$$ $$d(0) = 11.46$$ The estimated length of daylight on March 1 is 11.46 hours.
